title: The Menu-Screen Highway

features.name: horizon-box
features.desc-on-touch: The blue-grid line where the road stops being and starts being rendered; it does not recede—it waits at a fixed distance, the box you live inside.

features.name: menu-glyphs
features.desc-on-touch: Yellow letters that say CONTINUE / RESTART / QUIT; they have become environmental features; locals stand here to make choices that seem permanent but reset each dawn.

features.name: dithered-palms
features.desc-on-touch: Pixellated date palms that flicker between colour and static; they are the highway's attempt to render tropics on a 16-colour palette; they fail beautifully.

features.name: select-cursor
features.desc-on-touch: A blinking underline that you stand upon; it marks your location not as a coordinate but as a menu choice; you are always selected.

features.name: highway-vanish
features.desc-on-touch: Where the road narrows into an arch too small to enter; it suggests infinite depth but the road ends here; you have reached the inside edge of the simulation.

inscription: the highway renders itself as the software that displays it; place and screenshot are no longer distinguishable

file.name: Photo_ServiceStation_Dusk_NSW_1993_v2.jpg
file.note: the photograph now contains the highway it was taken from; the road is rendering itself in its own pixel-space; metadata timestamp: taken before the station opened / downloaded after it closed

exits.label: back through the dusk to the service station that exists and doesn't
deeper into the blue grid until the horizon closes and becomes a wall
up into the diagnostic render's domestic spaces, the interiors this exterior reflects
off the rendered road into the land underneath the grid
exits.to: NEW: the substrate below the menu-screen, where the actual earth waits unmapped
